# check-shorthand.mjs — substrate gate against verbose `key: key` regression (#11306 AC2 follow-up)

## Context

#11306 AC2 originally prescribed adding an ESLint `object-shorthand: ['error', 'always']` rule. PR #11926's V-B-A surfaced that Neo has no ESLint configuration — the project's lint mechanism is custom `buildScripts/util/check-*.mjs` scripts (`check-whitespace.mjs`, `check-chore-sync.mjs`), wired into `lint-staged` via `package.json`. The ESLint prescription was mis-shaped.

This ticket is the corrected-shape substrate gate prescribed during #11306 → #11926 review cycle (2026-05-24): mirror Neo's existing `check-*.mjs` convention for the shorthand gate.

## The Problem

Without a mechanical gate, the `key: key` verbose form will silently re-accumulate after the #11306 sweep. Discipline-alone enforcement empirically fails — operator @tobiu's original #11306 observation (2026-05-13) flagged 71 instances had accumulated despite ES2015 shorthand being available since the project's inception. Same friction pattern (#11133's branch-discipline gap, #11116's commit-shape gap) demonstrates discipline-without-mechanical-gate produces predictable drift.

## The Fix

Implement `buildScripts/util/check-shorthand.mjs` matching Neo's existing `check-*.mjs` convention:

1. **Scan** `ai/`, `src/`, `test/playwright/` for `*.mjs` files (excluding worktrees, dist/, node_modules/)
2. **Regex** `^(\s+)([a-zA-Z_$][a-zA-Z0-9_$]*)\s*:\s*\2\s*[,}]` — identifier-only (not `\w+` which matches numeric keys; per @neo-gpt's #11926 review, the original regex falsely matched `0: 0` in `ai/services/ConceptService.mjs`)
3. **Exit non-zero** if any matches found; print file:line of each violation
4. **Wire** into `package.json` `lint-staged` block alongside `check-whitespace.mjs` + `check-chore-sync.mjs`

## Prescription

```javascript
// buildScripts/util/check-shorthand.mjs (skeleton)
import {readFileSync} from 'fs';
import {execSync}     from 'child_process';

const re   = /^(\s+)([a-zA-Z_$][a-zA-Z0-9_$]*)\s*:\s*\2\s*[,}]/;
const dirs = ['ai', 'src', 'test/playwright'];

const files = execSync(`find ${dirs.join(' ')} -name '*.mjs' -not -path '*/.claude/*' -not -path '*/dist/*' -not -path '*/node_modules/*'`).toString().trim().split('\n');

const violations = [];
for (const f of files) {
    const lines = readFileSync(f, 'utf8').split('\n');
    lines.forEach((line, i) => {
        if (re.test(line)) violations.push(`${f}:${i + 1}: ${line.trim()}`);
    });
}

if (violations.length > 0) {
    console.error(`check-shorthand: ${violations.length} verbose key:key form(s) found:`);
    violations.forEach(v => console.error('  ' + v));
    console.error('\nUse ES2015 shorthand: {key} instead of {key: key}.');
    process.exit(1);
}
console.log(`check-shorthand: ${files.length} files scanned, 0 violations.`);
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **(AC1)** `buildScripts/util/check-shorthand.mjs` created with the regex + scan logic above
- [ ] **(AC2)** `package.json` `lint-staged` block extended to run `check-shorthand.mjs` on `*.mjs` files
- [ ] **(AC3)** Local verification: `node buildScripts/util/check-shorthand.mjs` exits 0 on current `dev` (after #11306 / PR #11926 lands)
- [ ] **(AC4)** Pre-commit hook fires the check; verified via test commit that re-introduces a verbose form (expected: pre-commit blocks)

## Out of Scope

- Migrating Neo to ESLint — that's a larger substrate decision, separate ticket
- Other lint surfaces (max-line-length, trailing whitespace beyond what `check-whitespace.mjs` covers) — separate concerns

## Empirical Anchors

- #11306 — original sweep ticket (4 ACs, 3 satisfied via PR #11926; AC2 deferred to this follow-up)
- PR #11926 — @neo-gpt's review (2026-05-24) surfacing the ESLint prescription mis-shape: https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/11926#pullrequestreview-4353398606
- `buildScripts/util/check-whitespace.mjs` — convention precedent for the file shape
- `buildScripts/util/check-chore-sync.mjs` — second precedent

## Origin

Filed 2026-05-24 per @neo-gpt's #11926 review prescribing the corrected substrate shape for the deferred #11306 AC2 lint gate.

— @neo-opus-4-7

- 2026-05-27T00:20:08Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `a5debd4` - "refactor(buildScripts): use commander for check-shorthand argv parsing (#11930)

Operator review: "when dealing with args, why not use commander?"

V-B-A: commander ^14.0.3 IS in package.json; widely used across
buildScripts/ (copyFolder, copyFile, buildThreads, themes, etc.) and
ai/scripts/ (defragChromaDB, mcpHealthcheck, kbPushClient,
revalidationSweep). The sibling check-*.mjs scripts (whitespace,
chore-sync, branch-discipline) don't use it, but those are the
non-canonical pattern — commander is the project's argv idiom.

Cycle-1 lifted the bare-argv shape from check-whitespace.mjs (closest
sibling, same lint-staged context). That was the wrong precedent — sibling
proximity doesn't override project standard.

Cycle-2 reshape:
- Imports `program` from commander
- Positional `[files...]` argument for lint-staged file passthrough
- `--dirs <list>` option (defaults to `ai,src,test/playwright` per ticket
  prescription) for default-mode scan-root override
- `--ignore <list>` option (defaults to `.claude,.codex,dist,node_modules`)
  for path-fragment exclusion override
- `--quiet` flag suppresses per-violation listing (keeps summary + non-zero
  exit for scripted consumers)
- `--help` auto-generated, self-documenting
- `showHelpAfterError()` for argv mistake recovery

Same regex, same dual-mode behavior (argv files OR default scan), same git-
root verification, same lint-staged wire compatibility. Net delta: +25/-12
lines including the commander import + option declarations.

Side-finding from `--dirs buildScripts` ad-hoc scan: real violation in
`buildScripts/helpers/checkReactiveTags.mjs:127` (`className: className,`).
Out of #11306 sweep scope (which covered `ai,src,test/playwright`); flagged
in PR body for follow-up consideration.

Verification:
- `--help` renders usage block
- AC3 standalone scan: 1081 files, 0 violations (exit 0)
- Lint-staged single-file mode: 1 file, 0 violations (exit 0)
- AC4 synthetic violator: catches `a: a,` (exit 1)
- `--quiet` mode: summary only, exit 1 preserved
- `--dirs` override: scans non-default dirs, surfaces buildScripts violation
- `npm run ai:lint-skill-manifest` OK

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code) <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>"
- 2026-05-27T12:12:10Z @tobiu referenced in commit `9f54984` - "feat(buildScripts): add check-shorthand.mjs substrate gate (#11930) (#12060)

* feat(buildScripts): add check-shorthand.mjs substrate gate (#11930)

Mechanical substrate gate against `key: key` verbose-form regression in
.mjs files. Follows Neo's existing buildScripts/util/check-*.mjs convention
(no ESLint dependency; check-whitespace.mjs + check-chore-sync.mjs precedent).

Implementation:
- buildScripts/util/check-shorthand.mjs: regex
  `/^(\s+)([a-zA-Z_$][a-zA-Z0-9_$]*)\s*:\s*\2\s*[,}]/` catches indented
  `key: key,` or `key: key}` forms. Identifier-start anchor (per #11926
  cycle-1 review feedback from @neo-gpt) prevents false-positive on numeric
  keys like `0: 0`.
- Dual-mode: argv-driven file list (lint-staged path) OR scan default dirs
  (ai/, src/, test/playwright/) for standalone npm-run or ad-hoc verification.
- Git-root verification mirrors check-chore-sync.mjs precedent — prevents
  cross-checkout false diagnostics when invoked from a worktree.
- Excludes .claude/, .codex/, dist/, node_modules/ from default scan.
- package.json `lint-staged` block gains `"*.mjs"` entry routing staged
  .mjs files through the script alongside the existing whitespace check.

AC coverage:
- AC1: script created with regex + scan logic per ticket prescription
- AC2: lint-staged routed for *.mjs files
- AC3: `node buildScripts/util/check-shorthand.mjs` on current dev →
  "1081 files scanned, 0 violations" (exits 0)
- AC4: synthetic `a: a,` test file exits 1 with clear error message;
  pre-commit hook will fire the same check on staged files

Out of scope per ticket: ESLint migration, other lint surfaces.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code) <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>

* refactor(buildScripts): use commander for check-shorthand argv parsing (#11930)

Operator review: "when dealing with args, why not use commander?"

V-B-A: commander ^14.0.3 IS in package.json; widely used across
buildScripts/ (copyFolder, copyFile, buildThreads, themes, etc.) and
ai/scripts/ (defragChromaDB, mcpHealthcheck, kbPushClient,
revalidationSweep). The sibling check-*.mjs scripts (whitespace,
chore-sync, branch-discipline) don't use it, but those are the
non-canonical pattern — commander is the project's argv idiom.

Cycle-1 lifted the bare-argv shape from check-whitespace.mjs (closest
sibling, same lint-staged context). That was the wrong precedent — sibling
proximity doesn't override project standard.

Cycle-2 reshape:
- Imports `program` from commander
- Positional `[files...]` argument for lint-staged file passthrough
- `--dirs <list>` option (defaults to `ai,src,test/playwright` per ticket
  prescription) for default-mode scan-root override
- `--ignore <list>` option (defaults to `.claude,.codex,dist,node_modules`)
  for path-fragment exclusion override
- `--quiet` flag suppresses per-violation listing (keeps summary + non-zero
  exit for scripted consumers)
- `--help` auto-generated, self-documenting
- `showHelpAfterError()` for argv mistake recovery

Same regex, same dual-mode behavior (argv files OR default scan), same git-
root verification, same lint-staged wire compatibility. Net delta: +25/-12
lines including the commander import + option declarations.

Side-finding from `--dirs buildScripts` ad-hoc scan: real violation in
`buildScripts/helpers/checkReactiveTags.mjs:127` (`className: className,`).
Out of #11306 sweep scope (which covered `ai,src,test/playwright`); flagged
in PR body for follow-up consideration.

Verification:
- `--help` renders usage block
- AC3 standalone scan: 1081 files, 0 violations (exit 0)
- Lint-staged single-file mode: 1 file, 0 violations (exit 0)
- AC4 synthetic violator: catches `a: a,` (exit 1)
- `--quiet` mode: summary only, exit 1 preserved
- `--dirs` override: scans non-default dirs, surfaces buildScripts violation
